overview.tagline: An upscale residential community set among wooded hills and pristine open space
overview.description: The Borough of Upper Saddle River is an affluent residential community in northwestern Bergen County, known for its large-lot zoning, wooded landscapes, and top-rated schools. With a one-acre minimum lot size and strict preservation of open space, the borough maintains a semi-rural character that attracts families seeking privacy, nature, and excellent education. Upper Saddle River is home to the renowned Northern Highlands Regional High School district.
overview.schoolDistrict: Upper Saddle River Public Schools / Northern Highlands Regional High School
highlights: Top-rated Northern Highlands Regional High School
One-acre minimum lot zoning preserves spacious, wooded character
Significant protected open space and nature preserves
Low-density residential community with excellent schools
Scenic setting with rolling hills and mature trees

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Upper Saddle River's one-acre minimum lot zoning and high-quality housing stock result in assessments that reflect the premium nature of the community.
realEstate.recentTrends: Upper Saddle River's real estate market remains strong, driven by demand for top-rated schools and spacious properties. Homes typically range from $800,000 to over $3 million, with newer construction and homes on larger lots commanding premium prices. Inventory is limited, keeping the market competitive.
neighborhoodNotes: West Saddle River Road — Established estates with large lots and mature landscaping
Lake Street area — Properties near natural areas and open space
Pleasant Avenue section — Well-maintained homes with a community feel
East Saddle River Road — Mix of classic and newer construction homes
Northern section — Larger properties bordering Mahwah

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided weekly by a contracted hauler for all residential properties.
details: Weekly trash collection on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected on a regular schedule for all residential properties.
details: Commingled recyclables collected on designated recycling days
Paper and cardboard collected separately
Yard waste collected seasonally by DPW
Recycling depot available at the DPW yard
Electronics recycling through Bergen County programs

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water and sewer service is provided by the borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water New Jersey
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Quarterly billing for water and sewer
Water quality reports published annually
Some properties in remote areas may have private wells

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits and zoning compliance.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Strict zoning enforcement including one-acre minimum lot size
Environmental review for projects near wetlands or waterways
Tree removal permits required for significant trees
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Parking in Upper Saddle River is primarily on private property, with limited public parking at borough facilities.
details: No metered parking in the borough
Parking available at Borough Hall and community buildings
Street parking limited on most borough roads
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Overnight parking restrictions may apply

additionalServices.title: Upper Saddle River Public Library
additionalServices.description: Community library offering books, digital resources, and programs.
details: Located at 245 Lake Street
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Community meeting room available

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for borough residents.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer camps
Adult and senior programs
Community events and holiday celebrations
Facilities at borough parks and fields

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Upper Saddle River enforces strict noise regulations to maintain the quiet, residential character of the community.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:30 AM and after 6:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to certain hours
Generator and mechanical equipment noise must comply with decibel limits
noise.source: Borough of Upper Saddle River Code

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in keeping with the borough's residential standards.
keyRules: Lawns and grounds must be maintained in neat condition
Property owners responsible for snow removal on sidewalks and driveways
Fences must comply with zoning setback and height requirements
Dead or hazardous trees must be removed
No storage of commercial vehicles or equipment on residential lots
propertyMaintenance.source: Borough of Upper Saddle River Code

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Upper Saddle River requires dog licensing and enforces animal control regulations.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed or under owner's control on public property
Pet waste must be picked up from public areas
Kennels and breeding operations subject to zoning restrictions
Wildlife feeding prohibited except for bird feeders
pets.source: Borough of Upper Saddle River Code

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is restricted on borough roads to maintain safety.
keyRules: No parking on many borough roads during specified hours
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
No commercial vehicles parked on residential properties visible from the road
Construction vehicles must park on work site property
All vehicles must be registered and in operable condition
parking.source: Borough of Upper Saddle River Code

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental of single-family homes is subject to borough zoning and code requirements.
keyRules: Rental properties must meet all building and safety codes
Short-term rentals are generally not permitted in residential zones
Accessory apartments subject to strict zoning approval
No subdivision of single-family lots for multiple units
Landlords must maintain properties to code standards
rentals.source: Borough of Upper Saddle River Code

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: Upper Saddle River has strong tree preservation ordinances to protect its wooded character.
keyRules: Permit required to remove trees over a specified caliper
Replacement planting required when significant trees are removed
Environmental Commission reviews tree removal applications
Protection of trees near waterways and wetlands is mandatory
other.source: Borough of Upper Saddle River Code

other.title: Open Space Preservation
other.summary: The borough actively preserves open space to maintain its natural character.
keyRules: Open space trust fund supports land acquisition and preservation
Conservation easements encouraged on large properties
Cluster development may be allowed to preserve natural areas
Wetland buffer zones strictly enforced
other.source: Borough of Upper Saddle River Code

schools.publicDistrict: Upper Saddle River Public Schools (K-8) / Northern Highlands Regional High School (9-12)

publicSchools.name: Bogert Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the borough with strong academics and enrichment programs

publicSchools.name: Cavallini Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school known for rigorous academics and extracurricular opportunities

publicSchools.name: Northern Highlands Regional High School
publicSchools.notes: Highly rated regional high school serving Upper Saddle River and Allendale; consistently ranked among NJ's top public high schools

privateSchools.name: Saddle River Day School
privateSchools.notes: Independent college-preparatory school located in nearby Saddle River

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 10 minutes

whyMoveHere: Top-rated Northern Highlands Regional High School
Spacious properties with one-acre minimum lots
Beautiful wooded setting with extensive open space preservation
Safe, family-oriented community with low crime
Strong property values and well-maintained neighborhoods
Proximity to nature preserves and outdoor recreation

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus / Drive to train station
commuteToNYC.trainStation: Ramsey Route 17 Station or Allendale Station (nearby)
commuteToNYC.notes: Upper Saddle River residents typically drive to nearby NJ Transit stations in Ramsey or Allendale for Main/Bergen County Line train service to Hoboken. Bus service via Route 165 is also available. The commute to Manhattan ranges from 55-80 minutes depending on mode and traffic conditions.

costOfLiving.summary: Upper Saddle River is an affluent community with a high cost of living driven primarily by housing costs. The community offers excellent value for families prioritizing top schools and a spacious lifestyle.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 70% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Outstanding public schools culminating in Northern Highlands High School
Large lots with wooded, private settings
Significant preserved open space and natural beauty
Very safe community with strong property values
Active recreation programs and community events
cons: High home prices with limited inventory
High property taxes typical of Bergen County
Longer commute to NYC compared to southern Bergen towns
Limited dining and entertainment options within the borough

restaurants.name: Biagio's Ristorante
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian dining popular with Upper Saddle River residents, located just south of the borough.

restaurants.name: The Brick House
restaurants.description: American restaurant and bar offering craft cocktails, creative American fare, and a lively atmosphere in nearby Wyckoff.

restaurants.name: Esty Street
restaurants.description: Contemporary farm-to-table restaurant in nearby Park Ridge offering seasonal menus and craft cocktails.

parks.name: Hopper Farm Park
parks.description: Borough park on preserved farmland featuring trails, open fields, and picnic areas with a connection to the community's agricultural heritage.
parks.address: Lake Street, Upper Saddle River, NJ 07458

parks.name: Saddle River County Park — Wild Duck Pond Area
parks.description: Bergen County park section with a scenic pond, walking trails, and wildlife observation opportunities.
parks.address: East Saddle River Road, Upper Saddle River, NJ 07458

parks.name: Bear Brook Park
parks.description: Quiet borough park with wooded trails, a stream, and natural areas for exploration.

parks.name: Borough Fields and Recreation Area
parks.description: Community sports fields and playground facilities used for youth sports and recreation programs.
parks.address: West Saddle River Road, Upper Saddle River, NJ 07458

events.name: Upper Saddle River Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with food, games, live music, and activities for families.

events.name: Memorial Day Ceremony
events.description: Annual ceremony honoring veterans and fallen service members at the borough memorial.
events.when: Memorial Day, last Monday in May

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Community holiday celebration with tree lighting, caroling, and refreshments.

attractions.name: Preserved Open Spaces
attractions.description: Upper Saddle River boasts hundreds of acres of preserved open space, wetlands, and nature areas, making it ideal for hiking, bird watching, and nature photography.

attractions.name: Historic Hopper-Goetschius House Museum
attractions.description: Historic Dutch Colonial stone house dating to the 18th century, preserved as a museum showcasing local history.
attractions.address: East Saddle River Road, Upper Saddle River, NJ 07458

shoppingAreas: Ramsey Main Street — Nearby dining and shops
Allendale downtown — Small shops and restaurants
Wyckoff shopping — Boutiques and dining along Franklin Avenue
